context: 1995 was a landmark year in Bollywood music. A.R. Rahman's breakthrough scores for Bombay and Rangeela fused electronic, Sufi, and classical traditions, permanently changing Hindi film music. Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge became the best-selling soundtrack of the year with 25+ million copies, and its songs became generational anthems. MTV India and Channel V were reshaping how music reached audiences beyond cinema halls.

tracks.title: Tujhe Dekha Toh Yeh Jaana Sanam
Yeh Bandhan Toh Pyar Ka Bandhan Hai
Ho Gaya Hai Tujhko Toh Pyar Sajna
